sentiment_score_definition: x <= -0.35: Bearish; -0.35 < x <= -0.15: Somewhat-Bearish; -0.15 < x < 0.15: Neutral; 0.15 <= x < 0.35: Somewhat_Bullish; x >= 0.35: Bullish
relevance_score_definition: 0 < x <= 1, with a higher score indicating higher relevance.

feed.title: Waste Connections Reports Strong Q4 And FY25 Results
feed.summary: Waste Connections, Inc. (WCN) reported robust financial results for the fourth quarter and full year ended December 31, 2025, showing significant revenue growth and profitability improvement. The company's revenue increased to $2.37 billion in Q4 and $9.47 billion for the full year 2025, with earnings per share rising to $1.01 and $4.17 respectively. WCN also announced an increase in its annual dividend to $1.295 per share for 2025.

feed.title: Vale’s Stock Soars as Goldman Sachs Boosts Price Target
feed.summary: Vale S.A.'s stock has risen by 3.88% following strong Q4 operational updates and positive commodity price expectations, notably iron ore production and copper. Goldman Sachs has upgraded Vale's price target, contributing to increased investor confidence despite some caution from Scotiabank regarding short-term market volatility. The company's financial indicators, including $38.06 billion in earnings and a 42% pre-tax profit margin, highlight its robust market position.

feed.title: MLM Q4 Deep Dive: Infrastructure, Data Center, and Cost Initiatives Headline Guidance Reset
feed.summary: Martin Marietta Materials (MLM) missed Q4 CY2025 revenue and adjusted EPS expectations, leading to a negative market response and a guidance reset. Management attributed the shortfall to softness in private construction but highlighted record profitability in core aggregates. The company's future outlook is shaped by public infrastructure investment, accelerating data center demand, and ongoing cost optimization initiatives, with residential and private nonresidential construction remaining headwinds.

feed.title: Citizens Financial Group (CFG) Declares Quarterly Dividend
feed.summary: Citizens Financial Group (CFG) has announced a quarterly dividend of $0.4609 per share for its Series H preferred stock, maintaining its previous payout level. The company projects a 10%-12% growth in net interest income and plans a share repurchase program ranging from $700 million to $850 million in 2026. While CFG shows strong financial health with a high Piotroski F-Score and robust free cash flow, its valuation metrics suggest potential overvaluation.

feed.title: GNRC Q4 Deep Dive: Data Center Momentum and Residential Headwinds Shape Outlook
feed.summary: Generac (GNRC) reported Q4 CY2025 results below market expectations for revenue and non-GAAP profit, with sales falling 11.6% year-on-year. Despite this, the stock reacted positively due to accelerating momentum in its commercial and industrial (C&I) segment, particularly from data center demand. The company anticipates mid-teens overall revenue growth in 2026, driven by continued strong data center demand and an expected recovery in residential sales.

feed.title: Intercontinental Exchange's Q4 Earnings Call: Our Top 5 Analyst Questions
feed.summary: Intercontinental Exchange (ICE) reported stronger-than-expected Q4 results, with revenue and non-GAAP EPS exceeding analyst estimates, driven by growth across its exchange, fixed income, and mortgage technology segments. Key questions from analysts focused on the outlook for mortgage technology, AI's impact on data and analytics, sustainability of futures trading volumes, and the timeline for resolving mortgage recurring revenue headwinds. The company is currently trading at $153.32, down from $164.85 before earnings.

feed.title: Coursera's Q4 Earnings Call: Our Top 5 Analyst Questions
feed.summary: Coursera's Q4 CY2025 results surpassed analyst expectations for revenue and adjusted EBITDA, driven by AI-powered innovations and international expansion. Despite strong performance, some financial metrics like Annual Recurring Revenue and Q1 EBITDA guidance fell short. The article highlights key analyst questions from the earnings call, focusing on platform fees, data advantage, international strategy, the Udemy merger, and enterprise account fluctuations.

feed.title: The 5 Most Interesting Analyst Questions From Cardinal Health’s Q4 Earnings Call
feed.summary: Cardinal Health reported strong Q4 results, exceeding Wall Street expectations in revenue, adjusted EPS, and EBITDA, driven by robust performance in its pharmaceutical and specialty solutions segment. The company raised its full-year adjusted EPS guidance. Key analyst questions focused on profit drivers, capital allocation, the impact of price decreases, and the evolving GLP-1 market.

feed.title: New Haven schools partner with General Dynamics Electric Boat to 'expand opportunities' for students
feed.summary: New Haven Public Schools has partnered with General Dynamics Electric Boat to expand educational and career opportunities for students, especially those in technical education and manufacturing pathways. This collaboration will bring Electric Boat employees into classrooms to mentor students and align curriculum with industry trends, preparing them for potential internships, apprenticeships, and full-time positions at the company. The initiative aims to connect education directly to career opportunities and strengthen Connecticut's workforce.

feed.title: MGM Q4 Deep Dive: Revenue Growth, Margin Pressure, and Strategic Focus on Digital Expansion
feed.summary: MGM Resorts International reported mixed Q4 CY2025 results, with revenue and non-GAAP EPS exceeding expectations, driven by Macau operations and high-end gaming, but adjusted EBITDA fell short due to margin pressures. The company's future strategy focuses on stabilizing Las Vegas operations, expanding digital ventures like BetMGM, and maintaining growth in Macau. Management emphasized cost controls, capital allocation, and leveraging technology to navigate competitive pressures and achieve solid top-line growth in 2026.

feed.title: Caprock Group LLC Buys Shares of 21,654 Leidos Holdings, Inc. $LDOS
feed.summary: Caprock Group LLC has acquired a new position in Leidos Holdings, Inc. (NYSE:LDOS), purchasing 21,654 shares valued at approximately $3,918,000 during the third quarter. Other institutional investors like Allianz Asset Management GmbH and Synergy Asset Management LLC also increased their stakes in the aerospace company. Insider selling activity was noted, with Elizabeth A. Porter selling 3,000 shares, while analysts maintain a "Moderate Buy" rating for Leidos with a consensus price target of $213.09.

feed.title: The Risk Factors to Watch Out For in Caterpillar Stock
feed.summary: This article highlights key risk factors for Caterpillar (CAT) stock, focusing on potential margin compression due to tariffs, deteriorating earnings quality indicated by cash flow lag, and escalating competitive pressure in the North American construction market. It references past stock performance during economic downturns and provides current financial metrics, suggesting a need for a diversified investment approach.

feed.title: Stuck With an Empty Factory, Ford Seeks a New Market
feed.summary: Ford is converting its idle battery factory in Glendale, Kentucky, which initially produced EV batteries, to now manufacture large-scale storage batteries for utilities, data centers, and homeowners. This strategic pivot aims to capitalize on the growing demand for energy storage, despite Ford's limited experience in this specific battery market and facing seasoned competitors. The move follows financial losses related to its previous EV battery venture and reflects a shift in market dynamics influenced by changing political policies regarding electric vehicles.

feed.title: Bernstein Maintains Outperform on Trimble Inc (TRMB) Feb 2026
feed.summary: Bernstein reiterated its Outperform rating for Trimble Inc. (TRMB) on February 11, 2026, signaling continued confidence in the stock despite an intraday decline of -3.95%. The firm maintained its positive view, suggesting TRMB should be on investors' shopping lists, but notably did not provide a new price target. Investors are advised to consider this maintained rating alongside company fundamentals, recent earnings, and broader market context, as it represents ongoing conviction rather than a fresh catalyst for the stock.

feed.title: Alamos Gold Inc. (NYSE:AGI) Emerges as a Prime GARP Candidate with Strong Growth and Sound Fundamentals
feed.summary: Alamos Gold Inc. (NYSE:AGI) is highlighted as a strong Growth at a Reasonable Price (GARP) candidate due to its robust fundamental profile, excellent health, profitability, and significant growth trajectory. The company boasts a high Profitability Rating of 8/10 and Health Rating of 7/10, coupled with a top-level Growth rating of 9/10, indicating strong past performance and future EPS and Revenue growth. Despite a seemingly high P/E ratio, its valuation is considered moderate when adjusted for growth and compared to industry peers, making it an attractive prospect for GARP investors.

feed.title: 5 Must-Read Analyst Questions From Mettler-Toledo's Q4 Earnings Call
feed.summary: Mettler-Toledo's Q4 results surpassed Wall Street expectations, with revenue of $1.13 billion and adjusted EPS of $13.36, driven by strong industrial and product inspection sales. Despite a positive Q4, management issued cautious Q1 2026 revenue guidance due to anticipated customer hesitancy and softness in certain market segments. Analysts focused on the conservative future outlook, the sustainability of retail growth, and the timing of onshoring opportunities during the earnings call.

feed.title: Molina Healthcare's Q4 Earnings Call: Our Top 5 Analyst Questions
feed.summary: Molina Healthcare reported disappointing Q4 results with significant margin pressures due to high medical costs and unexpected Medicaid adjustments, leading to a substantial EPS miss. Management addressed analyst concerns regarding Medicaid margins, contract attrition, cost trend assumptions, and the ROI of new contracts and M&A, expressing optimism for future recovery despite current challenges. Key focuses for upcoming quarters include Medicaid rate adjustments, the Florida CMS contract launch, and reducing Marketplace and MAPD exposure to stabilize margins.

feed.title: 5 Revealing Analyst Questions From Carrier Global's Q4 Earnings Call
feed.summary: Carrier Global's Q4 2025 earnings show continued softness in residential and light commercial HVAC markets, leading to missed revenue and EPS estimates. Despite double-digit growth in commercial HVAC and aftermarket segments, broader headwinds impacted margins. The article highlights key analyst questions and management's responses regarding margin dynamics, pricing, data center revenue, and inventory, setting expectations for future performance.

feed.title: The 5 Most Interesting Analyst Questions From Cardinal Health's Q4 Earnings Call
feed.summary: Cardinal Health's Q4 earnings surpassed Wall Street expectations with robust top-line and profit growth, driven by strong demand in its pharmaceutical and specialty solutions segment. The company also saw double-digit profit growth across all segments and raised its full-year Adjusted EPS guidance. Analyst questions on the earnings call focused on profit breakdown, segment outperformance, capital allocation, the impact of manufacturer price decreases, and the evolving GLP-1 market.

feed.title: The 5 Most Interesting Analyst Questions From Bristol-Myers Squibb's Q4 Earnings Call
feed.summary: Bristol-Myers Squibb's Q4 earnings call highlighted the strong performance of its growth portfolio, including Opdualag, Breyanzi, and Camzyos, which contributed significantly to positive results. Management addressed key analyst questions regarding upcoming clinical catalysts, business development priorities, cost-saving initiatives, and the impact of patent expirations on legacy brands like Eliquis. The company expects continued growth from its differentiated products and disciplined execution on cost savings in the coming quarters.

feed.title: The Top 5 Analyst Questions From Monolithic Power Systems's Q4 Earnings Call
feed.summary: Monolithic Power Systems (MPWR) reported a strong Q4, exceeding revenue and profit estimates due to robust demand in enterprise data, communications, and automotive markets. The article highlights key analyst questions from the earnings call, focusing on market segment trends, AI power share, automotive drivers, and gross margin dynamics. Management's responses indicate optimism for continued growth, driven by new AI data center solutions and an expanded automotive product lineup.

feed.title: Broadridge’s DLR platform reaches US$365 billion ADV for January
feed.summary: Broadridge Financial Solutions' Distributed Ledger Repo (DLR) platform processed US$365 billion in average daily trade volumes for repo transactions in January, totaling nearly US$7.3 trillion. This represents a 508 percent year-on-year increase, highlighting the growing institutional adoption of tokenized real-asset settlement. The company plans to expand the platform in 2026 to include intraday funding, enhanced collateral mobility, and a wider range of tokenized asset classes.

feed.title: The AES Corporation (AES) Positions for Long-Term Demand Growth in a Power-Hungry, AI-Driven World
feed.summary: The AES Corporation (AES) is strategically positioning itself for increasing power demand, especially in the AI sector, as evidenced by its recent partnership with Haven Safety AI and ongoing acquisition rumors involving GIP and EQT. Despite a recent downgrade by Barclays due to re-rating and leverage concerns, Jefferies reiterated a Hold rating with an increased price target, citing the potential acquisition and strong demand for renewable energy from tech giants. AES aims to transition to clean energy, but the article suggests other AI stocks might offer higher returns.

feed.title: The Progressive Corporation (PGR) In a Phase of Robust Growth Amid Advertising Efficiency
feed.summary: The Progressive Corporation (PGR) is experiencing robust growth, driven by improved advertising efficiency, despite some analyst price target cuts. While UBS maintained a Buy rating and Goldman Sachs reiterated a Buy rating due to strong December and Q4 results, highlighting increased net premiums and income, Keefe, Bruyette & Woods lowered its price target citing concerns about moderation in policies in force growth. Progressive remains a leading American insurance company for personal and commercial autos, motorcycles, boats, RVs, and homes.

feed.title: Verizon Communications Inc. (VZ) Balances Turnaround Initiatives With Ongoing Industry Competition
feed.summary: Verizon Communications Inc. (VZ) is undergoing a significant turnaround, including leadership changes, job cuts, and retail store conversions, under its new CEO. The company faces stiff industry competition, recently suing T-Mobile for alleged false advertising while Morgan Stanley raised its price target on Verizon due to improved financial discipline. Despite these efforts, some analysts suggest other AI stocks might offer greater upside potential with less risk.

feed.title: Block Graft Market Is Going to Boom | Regeneron Pharmaceuticals • Novartis • Pfizer
feed.summary: Coherent Market Insights has released a detailed research report on the Block Graft Market, projecting substantial growth from 2026 to 2033. The report analyzes key market trends, drivers, and challenges, providing insights into market size, revenue, and competitive landscape. It highlights major players like Regeneron Pharmaceuticals, Novartis, and Pfizer, and offers strategic recommendations for businesses in this sector.

feed.title: Phillips 66 (PSX) Shows Earnings Momentum Amid Strategic Portfolio Transformation
feed.summary: Phillips 66 (PSX) demonstrated strong financial performance in Q4 2025 and the full year, driven by a rebound in US refining margins and strategic portfolio changes. The company reported substantial earnings of $2.9 billion in Q4 and $4.4 billion for the full year, alongside record natural gas transportation and fractioning volumes. CEO Mark Lashier highlighted 2025 as a transformative year, marked by divestitures and acquisitions to enhance Phillips 66's core assets and competitive position.

feed.title: UnitedHealth Group Incorporated (UNH) Strengthens Healthcare Platform Through Optum Expansion
feed.summary: UnitedHealth Group (UNH) is strengthening its healthcare platform through the expansion of Optum Health, a division expected to generate over $13.2 billion in operating earnings. Despite a cut in its price target by Mizuho, analysts like Piper Sandler maintain an Outperform rating, citing confidence in Optum's potential for upside. The company reported solid Q4 and full-year 2025 results with increased revenues, demonstrating momentum in serving stakeholders and improving performance.

feed.title: Candriam S.C.A. Has $2.87 Million Position in Becton, Dickinson and Company $BDX
feed.summary: Candriam S.C.A. decreased its stake in Becton, Dickinson and Company (BDX) by 33.7% in Q3, now holding 15,305 shares worth approximately $2.865 million. BDX recently completed a spin-off of its biosciences & diagnostics unit, becoming a pure-play medical technology company and reporting strong Q1 FY2026 sales and EPS. Despite mixed analyst sentiment, with an average "Hold" rating and $196 price target, the company declared a quarterly dividend of $1.05 and authorized a $10 million share repurchase plan.

feed.title: Candriam S.C.A. Makes New Investment in Mohawk Industries, Inc. $MHK
feed.summary: Candriam S.C.A. has initiated a new position in Mohawk Industries (NYSE:MHK) by purchasing 23,959 shares valued at approximately $3.09 million. Despite this new investment, analysts maintain a "Hold" rating for Mohawk with a consensus price target of $129.78, reflecting caution due to recent downgrades and trimmed EPS estimates for 2026 and 2027 by Zacks. Insiders have recently been net sellers of the stock, with Suzanne L. Helen selling 2,700 shares in December and total insider sales amounting to 14,485 shares worth about $1.6 million over the last three months.

feed.title: Candriam S.C.A. Sells 9,163 Shares of Align Technology, Inc. $ALGN
feed.summary: Candriam S.C.A. reduced its stake in Align Technology (NASDAQ:ALGN) by 15.7%, selling 9,163 shares and retaining 49,263 shares valued at approximately $6.17 million. Despite mixed analyst ratings, with a consensus "Hold" and an average price target of $195.92, Align Technology recently surpassed quarterly earnings and revenue estimates. The company reported $3.29 EPS against an expected $2.99 and $1.05 billion in revenue against an estimated $1.03 billion, representing a 5.3% year-over-year revenue increase.

feed.title: Candriam S.C.A. Sells 8,099 Shares of Fair Isaac Corporation $FICO
feed.summary: Candriam S.C.A. significantly reduced its stake in Fair Isaac Corporation (NYSE:FICO) by 79.7% in Q3, selling 8,099 shares and retaining 2,067 shares valued at $3.09 million. This partial divestment occurred as FICO exceeded earnings expectations, reporting an EPS of $7.33 against an estimated $7.08, and revenue of $766.0 million, a 16.4% year-over-year increase. Analysts maintain a "Moderate Buy" consensus rating for FICO, with an average price target of $2,071.43, even as the stock has recently traded down 1.4%.

feed.title: Candriam S.C.A. Lowers Position in Simon Property Group, Inc. $SPG
feed.summary: Candriam S.C.A. significantly reduced its stake in Simon Property Group, Inc. (SPG) by 50.4% in the third quarter, ending with 13,798 shares valued at $2.59 million. Despite this, other institutional investors increased their holdings. Simon Property Group reported an EPS beat, increased revenue year-over-year, authorized a $2.0 billion share repurchase plan, and declared a quarterly dividend of $2.20 per share.

feed.title: Kimberly-Clark Corporation (KMB) Receives Boost on Kenvue Acquisition Push as Evercore ISI Warns of Competition Pressure
feed.summary: Kimberly-Clark Corporation's shareholders have approved the acquisition of Kenvue, aiming to create a combined health and wellness powerhouse by merging brands like Huggies and Kotex with Aveeno and Tylenol. This development comes as Evercore ISI lowered KMB's price target due to intensifying competition in the US diaper market, particularly after the end of Costco exclusivity and Walmart introducing new imported diaper brands. Evercore ISI anticipates significant pressure on Kimberly-Clark's diaper profit pools, more so than its competitors.

feed.title: Baader Bank Aktiengesellschaft Has $851,000 Stock Position in Endeavour Silver Corporation $EXK
feed.summary: Baader Bank Aktiengesellschaft significantly increased its stake in Endeavour Silver (NYSE:EXK) by 369% in Q3, now holding 108,520 shares valued at $851,000. Despite silver price increases, Endeavour Silver's profit remains elusive, with a negative P/E ratio of -36.39. Analysts generally maintain a "Buy" rating on EXK, with an average price target of $13.88.

feed.title: Bristol-Myers Squibb Company (BMY) Growth Portfolio Driving Earnings Growth
feed.summary: Bristol-Myers Squibb (BMY) reported strong fourth-quarter and fiscal 2025 results, with revenues reaching $12.5 billion and $48.2 billion respectively, driven by a 16-17% increase in their Growth Portfolio. Analysts at Bernstein SocGen Group and Wells Fargo reiterated their ratings and raised price targets, citing the strength of BMY's growth portfolio, particularly Eliquis, and upcoming pipeline readouts as key drivers for future performance. CEO Christopher Boerner emphasized the company's differentiated pipeline and strong core business, projecting industry-leading growth into the 2030s.

feed.title: Electronic Arts Inc. $EA Shares Sold by Envestnet Asset Management Inc.
feed.summary: Envestnet Asset Management Inc. reduced its stake in Electronic Arts (NASDAQ:EA) by 35.1% in the third quarter, selling 94,817 shares and now holding 175,215 shares valued at $35.34 million. Insider selling also occurred, with CEO Andrew Wilson and Vijayanthimala Singh disposing of shares worth about $24.94 million in the last quarter. Despite this, other institutional investors increased their positions, and analysts maintain a consensus "Hold" rating with an average target price of $186.95 for EA.

feed.title: Verizon Communications Inc. (VZ) Balances Turnaround Initiatives With Ongoing Industry Competition
feed.summary: Verizon Communications Inc. (VZ) is undergoing significant turnaround initiatives under new CEO Dan Schulman, including executive changes and job cuts, while also engaging in competitive industry disputes. Despite these internal changes and external challenges, Morgan Stanley recently raised its price target for Verizon, acknowledging its balanced approach to market promotions and financial discipline. The company, a major technology and communications provider, is also litigating against T-Mobile over alleged false advertising claims.

feed.title: Microchip Technology Q4 2025 Earnings Beat Estimates, Provides Strong 2026 Outlook
feed.summary: Microchip Technology exceeded analyst expectations for its Q4 2025 earnings, reporting $1.19 billion in revenue and $0.44 adjusted EPS, driven by strong growth in automotive, industrial, networking, data center, and FPGA product lines. The company provided optimistic Q1 2026 revenue guidance of $1.26 billion and adjusted EPS of $0.50, and management indicated that distribution inventory correction is largely complete, though they will prioritize debt reduction over share buybacks.

feed.title: 5 Must-Read Analyst Questions From Huntington Ingalls's Q4 Earnings Call
feed.summary: Huntington Ingalls (HII) reported strong Q4 revenue and EPS, exceeding analyst expectations, driven by improved shipbuilding throughput. Despite the positive top-line growth, the market responded negatively due to concerns about margin progression and the sustainability of recent gains, as management acknowledged ongoing cost pressures. Analysts' questions focused on productivity sustainability, margin improvement, and strategic investments in areas like unmanned technologies, highlighting key challenges and catalysts for future performance.

feed.title: Hilton Nets $298 Million in Q4 Earnings
feed.summary: Hilton Worldwide Holdings reported a net income of $298 million for the fourth quarter and $1.461 billion for the full year. The company added 26,000 rooms in Q4, bringing the total to 97,000 for the year, and projects net income for 2026 to be between $1.982 billion and $2.011 billion. Hilton also expanded its portfolio with new brands and partnerships, driving confidence in future net unit growth.

feed.title: Kimberly-Clark Corporation (KMB) Receives Boost on Kenvue Acquisition Push as Evercore ISI Warns of Competition Pressure
feed.summary: Kimberly-Clark Corporation (KMB) shareholders have approved the acquisition of Kenvue, forming a combined health and wellness company. This merger will integrate KMB's brands like Huggies and Kotex with Kenvue's Aveeno, Band-Aid, and Tylenol. However, Evercore ISI has lowered KMB's price target due to concerns over stiff competition in the US diaper market, particularly after the end of exclusivity at Costco and increased competition from Walmart.

feed.title: Sumitomo Mitsui Financial Group Inc $SMFG Shares Sold by Envestnet Asset Management Inc.
feed.summary: Envestnet Asset Management Inc. reduced its stake in Sumitomo Mitsui Financial Group (NYSE:SMFG) by 13.3% in the third quarter, selling 307,346 shares and retaining 1,995,065 shares valued at approximately $33.4 million. Despite missing quarterly EPS estimates, the stock is trading near a one-year high and currently holds an average analyst "Hold" rating. Other institutional investors like Fisher Asset Management and Orion Portfolio Solutions significantly increased their positions.

feed.title: Gartner, Inc. $IT Shares Sold by Envestnet Asset Management Inc.
feed.summary: Envestnet Asset Management Inc. reduced its stake in Gartner, Inc. by 38.7% in the third quarter, selling 78,268 shares and now holding 124,143 shares valued at approximately $32.63 million. The company reported strong quarterly earnings, beating EPS expectations, and institutional ownership remains high at 91.51%. Analysts currently have a "Hold" rating on Gartner's stock with an average target price of $190.70.

feed.title: Envestnet Asset Management Inc. Sells 21,397 Shares of Extra Space Storage Inc $EXR
feed.summary: Envestnet Asset Management Inc. recently reduced its stake in Extra Space Storage Inc (NYSE:EXR) by 8.5% in the third quarter, offloading 21,397 shares. Despite this sale, several other major institutions significantly increased their positions, leading to 99.11% of EXR being institutionally owned. Analysts currently hold a consensus "Hold" rating on the stock with an average target price of $144.67, and the company offers an attractive dividend yield of approximately 4.5%.

feed.title: Envestnet Asset Management Inc. Reduces Holdings in CMS Energy Corporation $CMS
feed.summary: Envestnet Asset Management Inc. significantly reduced its stake in CMS Energy Corporation by 60% in the third quarter of last year, selling over 623,000 shares. Despite this reduction, other institutional investors increased their holdings, and CMS Energy reported strong quarterly earnings, raising its full-year 2026 guidance and increasing its quarterly dividend. Wall Street analysts maintain a "Moderate Buy" consensus rating for the company.

feed.title: Envestnet Asset Management Inc. Cuts Stake in Sterling Infrastructure, Inc. $STRL
feed.summary: Envestnet Asset Management Inc. reduced its stake in Sterling Infrastructure (NASDAQ:STRL) by 12.1% in Q3, selling 13,544 shares, though institutional ownership remains high at 80.95%. Despite this, other major institutional investors like State Street Corp and Invesco Ltd. significantly increased their positions. The company has a "Moderate Buy" consensus rating from analysts with a target price of $453.00, and its board recently authorized a $400 million share buyback program.

feed.title: BIP Wealth LLC Raises Stock Position in NVIDIA Corporation $NVDA
feed.summary: BIP Wealth LLC significantly increased its stake in NVIDIA Corporation by 112.4% during Q3, now holding shares worth $11.235 million. Several other institutional investors also adjusted their positions in NVIDIA, with collective institutional ownership reaching 65.27%. The article also details recent insider stock sales, price performance, a dividend announcement, and analyst ratings, which largely suggest a "Buy" rating for NVDA.

feed.title: Envestnet Asset Management Inc. Has $34.87 Million Stock Holdings in Huntington Ingalls Industries, Inc. $HII
feed.summary: Envestnet Asset Management Inc. increased its stake in Huntington Ingalls Industries (HII) by 7.4% in the third quarter, now holding 121,116 shares valued at $34.87 million, representing 0.31% ownership. Institutional investors collectively own 90.46% of the company. Despite HII beating quarterly earnings expectations with $4.04 EPS on $3.48 billion revenue, analysts maintain a "Hold" rating with an average price target of $352.88, and insider selling by two VPs has been noted.

feed.title: Alphabet Inc. $GOOGL Stock Holdings Trimmed by Cambiar Investors LLC
feed.summary: Cambiar Investors LLC reduced its stake in Alphabet Inc. (NASDAQ:GOOGL) by 33.3% during the third quarter, selling over 112,000 shares, though Alphabet still represents 2.5% of their holdings. Other institutional investors like Financial Gravity Companies Inc. and Pilgrim Partners Asia Pte Ltd also adjusted their positions. Alphabet's stock recently opened at $310.96, with a market cap of $3.75 trillion, and analysts maintain a "Moderate Buy" consensus rating with a target price of $361.64.
